question: customer is asking how to set up text message verification for their Microsoft authenticator account
keywords: I want to set up a different method
reference_answer: To set up text message verification, the customer should click "I want to set up a different method", choose "Phone" as the method, click "Confirm", enter their cell phone number, and then enter the six-digit code received via text message to verify their login.

question: customer is asking what to do if they receive a phishing email that impersonates a department head and is sent to their department.
reference_answer: If a customer receives a phishing email that impersonates a department head and is sent to their department, they should forward the message to InformationSecurity@newpaltz.edu.

question: customer is asking about a fraudulent email with the subject 'SCHEDULED SYSTEM DOWNTIME - 1st June 2017' that asks them to click a link and provide their username and password.
reference_answer: The email with the subject 'SCHEDULED SYSTEM DOWNTIME - 1st June 2017' is fraudulent. It attempts to trick recipients into clicking a link and providing their username and password. Red flags include no company information, a required upgrade notification, a fraudulent link, and poor grammar.

question: customer is asking how to log in and authorize their EquatIO account on Windows
reference_answer: To log in and authorize your EquatIO account on Windows, first search for and select the EquatIO app from the Windows Home Button menu. Then, select Microsoft as the authentication method, enter your Outlook email address, and follow the prompts to enter your Outlook account email address and password. Upon successful entry, you should see an authorization affirmation screen and the EquatIO toolbar at the bottom of your screen.

question: customer is asking how to edit automatically generated captions in Panopto
reference_answer: To edit automatically generated captions in Panopto, navigate to the video, select the 'Captions' tab, click 'Edit', and then make your desired changes. Remember to save your edits.

question: customer is asking how to set the language on a PDF document in Adobe Acrobat Pro DC
reference_answer: To set the language of a PDF document, open the file in Adobe Acrobat Pro DC, go to File > Properties > Advanced. Under the Reading Options section, select the appropriate language from the dropdown menu.

question: customer is asking about the hours of operation for the IT Service Desk during the fall and spring semesters
reference_answer: During the Fall and Spring Semesters, the IT Service Desk is open Monday - Thursday from 8:00am to 8:00pm, and Friday from 8:00am to 5:00pm. On Saturdays and Sundays, the hours are 12:00pm to 5:00pm.

question: customer is asking about the hours of operation for the IT Service Desk
reference_answer: During the Fall and Spring Semesters, the IT Service Desk is open Monday - Thursday from 8:00am to 8:00pm, and Friday from 8:00am to 5:00pm. On Saturdays and Sundays, they are open from 12:00pm to 5:00pm. During the Summer Semester, hours are Monday - Friday from 8:00am to 4:00pm, and they are closed on Saturdays and Sundays.

question: customer is asking about the availability of laptop loaners for students
reference_answer: The IT Service Desk has laptop loaners available for students with all necessary applications installed.

question: customer is asking how to display a personal Apple device wirelessly in EG 113
reference_answer: To wirelessly display a personal Apple device through the Apple TV in EG 113, press the APPLE TV button on the Crestron Control Panel and refer to the 'How To Use Apple TV' guide.

question: customer is having trouble getting the document camera to display in their Extended Virtual Classroom and wants to know how to troubleshoot.
reference_answer: If the document camera is not displaying, the customer should first turn it on by pressing its power button. Then, they need to double click the document camera icon on the desktop. If issues persist, they should perform a visual observation to ensure the USB cable is connected between the document camera and the computer.

question: customer is asking where the computer lab is located in the Humanities building that has Mac computers
reference_answer: There are no Mac computers in the Humanities building. The Humanities building has computer labs in HUM 107 and HUM 301, both of which have Windows operating systems.

question: customer is asking how to enable macro usage on their computer
reference_answer: To allow a user to use macros, add them to the MacroUsers group in Active Directory Users & Computers, run 'gpupdate /force' in cmd or ps (as admin), and then have the user reboot their computer. If issues persist, check if the MacrosBySecGroup policy is applied using 'gpresult /r'. If filtered out, re-verify group membership, run gpupdate /force again (user context and admin context), and reboot.

question: customer is asking how to create a new asset in the system
reference_answer: All new assets must be created in IMS Assets. Do not create any new IMS related assets in Assets/CIs.

question: customer is asking how to run software that requires access escalation on the IMS system
reference_answer: To run software that requires access escalation on the IMS system, right-click the program and select "Run as Admin". Use the credentials username - .\IMSTECH and your password for the UAC window. The ".\" before IMSTECH ensures it uses the local user account.

question: customer is having trouble connecting to eduroam and is being prompted for their username and password to make changes to their Certificate Trust Settings
keywords: login to your Mac OS desktop or laptop
reference_answer: When prompted for your Username and Password to make changes to your Certificate Trust Settings, enter the username and password you use to log in to your Mac OS desktop or laptop when turning on the computer, not your email address or SUNY New Paltz account details.

question: customer is asking what VDI is and what it allows them to do
reference_answer: VDI (Virtual Desktop Infrastructure) is a service that allows users to remotely access a variety of software programs provided by the college. The university uses the VMWare VDI system, available at vdi.newpaltz.edu.

question: customer is asking how to save their work when using the VMware Horizon View Client on their Android device
reference_answer: When working in the virtual environment using the VMware Horizon View Client, all work must be saved to a USB drive or to cloud storage. Any work not saved to an external source will be lost when the session is shut down.

question: customer is asking about the deadline for social media accessibility compliance
reference_answer: All official social media accounts must adhere to ADA Title II regulations and WCAG 2.1 Level AA standards, with a compliance deadline of April 24, 2026.

question: customer is asking about the grace period for accessing services after graduation
reference_answer: Once a student is no longer enrolled in courses, they will continue to have access to services for a grace period lasting several months, until the mid-point of the following Fall or Spring semester. Access to my.newpaltz.edu will be retained, but other services will be removed if the student does not register for further courses.

question: customer is asking if they can take their university-owned laptop out of the country
reference_answer: University-owned computing devices must not be taken out of the country without the explicit approval of either the Chief Information Officer or the Information Security Officer, in addition to the Chair, Director, Dean, etc, of the owning department.

question: customer is asking how to search for emails containing specific phrases or words within Outlook.
reference_answer: In Outlook, you can search for one or more words, a person's name, an email address, or a phrase surrounded by quotes in the search box at the top. For example, searching for "New Paltz" will find emails with those two words together.

question: customer is asking about the passing score requirement for the module tests in the Foundational Certification Training
reference_answer: To earn Foundational Certification, all three module tests must be passed with a score of 80% or higher. Upon passing a module test with 80% or above, a badge is awarded in the Awards area of the Brightspace course. Earning all three badges results in a certificate valid for two years.

question: customer is asking how to submit a Panopto video to a Brightspace assignment
reference_answer: A video demonstrating how to submit a Panopto video to an assignment is available at https://newpaltz.hosted.panopto.com/Panopto/Pages/Viewer.aspx?id=89a30542-1a30-435e-af0c-af3f00de9f10.

question: A customer is asking how to approve a workflow step for a classroom technology issue, and they also want to know where to find more information about AV technology.
reference_answer: To approve a workflow step, go to support.newpaltz.edu, click 'My Tickets', then 'My Approvals', click on the 'Step' for the item, and then click 'Approve'. For more information about Audio Visual technology, they can walk in to the Service Desk in Humanities 103 or call 845-257-HELP (4357).

question: A customer is asking if they can store personal photos on their university-provided cloud storage, and what the university's responsibility is if those photos are accessed by others.
reference_answer: University cloud storage services are subject to freedom of information laws and litigation, and personal files stored there may be accessible by others at the University with proper approval. Therefore, personal files unrelated to university work should not be stored on University devices or services, and are not governed by the best effort policy.

question: A customer is asking about setting up for telecommuting and wants to know if they can use their personal device to make and receive calls, and what they need to do to ensure their device is secure for campus resources.
reference_answer: When telecommuting, you can use Jabber to make and receive calls from your computer, which is an alternative to forwarding calls from your office phone. For security when connecting to campus resources, you must ensure you have active and updated Antivirus software on your device, and remember that the IT department only supports campus-owned devices, software, and accounts, so personal devices may have limitations.
